title: Level 126
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 126: "a much-needed rest"}} {{Background|file=Blue-clouds-day-fluffy-53594.jpg}} {{CSS|content= /* Page */ .page { border: solid 4px rgba(255, 255, 255, 1); margin-top: 12px; position: relative; }} {{RecommendedAudioNest|audio={{Audio|round=yes|file=The dream that ends too soon - gakhed.mp3|align=center|loop=true|volume=0.6}}|text=enable page ambience for best experience:}}
here, i didn't find myself as cautious as i usually am upon entering a new level. it was nothing out of the ordinary, fitting into the likes of [[:Category:Sky|other sky-suspended domains]] i've encountered along my trek since noclipping here, but i found myself less worried about the possibility of some bloodthirsty beast or mental anomaly taking hold of me when i dared to lose my guard. it felt like i could breathe for once; both figuratively, with the weight of setting doom lifting itself off my back, and literally, with an air that rejuvenated my lungs upon each inhale. i don't know how to put it other than that i'll let myself indulge. i can't afford to take chances with my sanity any longer, and i could bear the humility of losing myself here if this ends up being some trap. whoever finds this note: take this as a factor to your assumptions of this place, because i won't be here to find you again. i'll be long gone either way.
{{Class 5|hexagon=❂|color-h=230|color-s=100%|color-l=80%|class=Xi|safety=Safe|security=Secure|entity=Psychological Benefits}} ---- {{SimpleHeader|header=OVERVIEW}}
[[File:Skypath.png|250x250px|right|link=]]
'''Level 126''' is a network of cobble garden tiles remaining in place over a surrounding cloud-speckled sky. The tile paths themselves have a similar thickness to paper, though breaking through the material requires power impossible to execute by raw human strength. The tiles are aligned in a stuttered square grid with patches of grass commonly poking out from between. Chairs, tables, and bed-like structures made of wicker straw are scattered about the paths. One cannot move these articles of furniture from their set placement. The shadows of furniture contradict each other; light permeates the level without a source. Despite the ever-extending sky below, all anxious feelings of falling from such heights are nullified. No attempts to jump from the paths have been successful, whether intentional or not. Upon entry, one will forget any ideas previously memorized regarding falling into the sky below, and one never loses their footing when walking. Any thoughts within the level regarding jumping from the paths will, in some way, be forgotten, and the wanderer in question will ultimately decide to remain on the solid ground. As such, it is unknown what happens if someone -- or any object, for the matter -- falls, though it is presumed that they fall endlessly. Although entities do exist in '''Level 126''', it is [[Phenomenon 15|impossible to encounter another wanderer]]. All who enter the level are placed in personal finite instances of the terrain, with no unique path formations shared between any two wanderers who have documented their experiences. The sky retains a set pattern of clouds between all accounts, however, suggesting that all path clusters exist in one space (albeit separated immeasurably far from one another). To emphasize, wanderers do not feel anxiety regarding heights when in this level, nor can they fall. Wanderers who encounter resting spaces (such as wicker chairs or bedframes) will feel comfortable in sitting on them. Upon sitting, the sight of '''Level 126''''s sky will grant respite from the instinctual fear one acquires within the Backrooms' environment; tensed muscles relax, breathing slows to a regular pace, and mental decline from constant alertness begins to heal. The effect remains temporarily after rising from the seat. {{SimpleHeader|header=ENTITIES}}
'''CLUTTERFLIES'''
[[File:Clutterfly.jpg|250x250px|right|link=]]
'''Clutterflies''' are non-uniform figures vaguely resembling butterflies with vibrantly shaded wings. No commonality exists in their forms between moments, as they consist of illusiform masses that constantly warp between blinks and focuses. There is sensation upon touching a '''Clutterfly''', though contact with matter has selective effect on its body. One could easily move their hand through a '''Clutterfly''' as if it were air. '''Clutterflies''' otherwise behave as typical insects do; resting for prolonged periods in one place and skittishly taking flight upon sensing loud noises or disturbance. They often rest near dense patches of grass peering out from gaps in the tiles, awkwardly clambering through the vegetation or clustering together in the shade of tables. When not directly sensing threatening behavior, '''Clutterflies''' will gather along the arms, shoulders, and heads of wanderers (this behavior remaining unexplained).
{{SimpleHeader|header=ENTRANCES & EXITS}}
'''ENTRANCES'''
* Sleeping on a couch in one of [[Level 125]]'s houses will cause one to awake in this level. * Templelike regions of [[Level 8]] will transition to this level. * [[Object 38|Doom Locks]] can open to this level once completed.
'''EXITS'''
At the end of a path, there may rarely be a computer terminal with cables extending indefinitely below. Entering any combination of username and password on the login screen will cause one's surroundings to shift into that of [[Level 132]], with the following exceptions: * If one enters "SUFFOCATIONNOBREATHING" as the username, the surroundings will instead shift to that of [[Level 127]]. * If one enters "TREACHERY" as the username, the surroundings will shift to that of [[Level 5.1]]. * If one enters "APRILSBLOOM" as the username, the surroundings will shift to that of [[Level 150]]. * If one enters "ABC124" as the username, the surroundings will shift to that of [[Level 499]]. * If one enters "ADMIN" as the password, the surroundings will shift to that of [[The Beginning]]. The preceding "exception" usernames were found written on sticky notes pasted to the terminal's sides.
the backrooms taught me to be wary of anything too good to be true. that any lone surplus of supplies could be a [[Dossier on the Common Raider|raider]]'s lure, or that a seemingly serene and idyllic level could be a facade masking its deadly nature. there's been so many false promises of clear skies after the thunderstorms i've lasted through that i've become depressingly weary. my hope and ambition to find a good ending for myself has only declined, and there once seemed to be no end to that crashing hope. this place feels different somehow. not like the "trap levels" with even offerings of paradise that feel viscerally wrong to look upon, but something actually real. something that wants to offer sanctity with all its heart. maybe these paths ahead of me aren't simply another trail directly into the gullet of hell, but rather a sincere break from the terrors the rest of the backrooms has to behold.
